issue_title: Support writing 16-bit color channels for PNG
issue_body:
I'm trying to transition away from MATLAB and imageio has been incredibly helpful. However, I need to work with 64-bit PNGs (16-bit color channels and 16-bit alpha channel) and although it looks like reading 16-bit channels is supported, writing is not supported.

I created some 64-bit pngs in MATLAB using the following code:

    img_out = zeros(256, 256, 3, 'uint16');
    img_alpha = zeros(256, 256, 'uint16');
    color_grad = uint16(reshape(0:2^16-1, 256, []));
    img_out(:, :, 1) = color_grad;
    img_out(:, :, 2) = rot90(color_grad, 1);
    img_out(:, :, 3) = rot90(color_grad, 2);
    img_out_alpha = rot90(color_grad, 3);

    fprintf('Write unique values: R=%u, G=%u, B=%u, A=%u\n', ...
            length(unique(img_out(:, :, 1))), ...
            length(unique(img_out(:, :, 2))), ...
            length(unique(img_out(:, :, 3))), ...
            length(unique(img_out_alpha)))
    imwrite(img_out, '64bit_MATLAB.png', 'Alpha', img_out_alpha, 'BitDepth', 16)
    
    [img_in, ~, img_in_alpha] = imread('64bit_MATLAB.png');
    fprintf('MATLAB PNG unique values: R=%u, G=%u, B=%u, A=%u\n', ...
            length(unique(img_in(:, :, 1))), ...
            length(unique(img_in(:, :, 2))), ...
            length(unique(img_in(:, :, 3))), ...
            length(unique(img_in_alpha)))

This gives me the following output:

> Write unique values: R=65536, G=65536, B=65536, A=65536
> MATLAB PNG unique values: R=65536, G=65536, B=65536, A=65536

If I try the same with imageio in Python:

    import imageio
    import numpy as np

    img_out = np.zeros((256, 256, 4), dtype=np.uint16)
    color_grad = np.reshape(np.arange(2**16), (256,-1))
    img_out[:, :, 0] = color_grad
    img_out[:, :, 1] = np.rot90(color_grad, 1)
    img_out[:, :, 2] = np.rot90(color_grad, 2)
    img_out[:, :, 3] = np.rot90(color_grad, 3)

    print('Write unique values: R={}, G={}, B={}, A={}'.format(
        len(set(img_out[:, :, 0].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_out[:, :, 1].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_out[:, :, 2].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_out[:, :, 3].flatten().tolist()))))
    imageio.imwrite('64bit_imageio.png', img_out)

    img_in = imageio.imread('64bit_imageio.png')
    print('imageio PNG unique values: R={}, G={}, B={}, A={}'.format(
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 0].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 1].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 2].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 3].flatten().tolist()))))

I get the following:

> Write unique values: R=65536, G=65536, B=65536, A=65536
> imageio PNG unique values: R=256, G=256, B=256, A=256

To confirm, imageio is able to read the 64-bit PNG:

    img_in = imageio.imread('64bit_MATLAB.png')
    print('MATLAB PNG unique values: R={}, G={}, B={}, A={}'.format(
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 0].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 1].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 2].flatten().tolist())),
        len(set(img_in[:, :, 3].flatten().tolist()))))

> MATLAB PNG unique values: R=65536, G=65536, B=65536, A=65536

Any idea how difficult this would be to implement?

issue_title: Deprecating support for Python 2.6
issue_body:
Separate issued branched off from [#337](https://github.com/SoCo/SoCo/issues/337#issuecomment-367275787).

Python 2.7 is the last supported 2.x version and requires some campatability code (#337). Python 2.7 will be maintained until 2020 (https://pythonclock.org/). 

This issue is to discuss our plans for supporting 2.7 for SoCo.

issue_title: The nt counting in dna.py should treat N symbols as gaps
issue_body: The `compareDNAReads` function in `dna.py` treats `N` symbols just like any other ambiguous symbol, even though it's completely non-specific. As a result, we can have what looks like a very good match whereas in fact the two sequences have long runs of `N` symbols. I think it would be better to treat `N` as a gap. It's essentially the same thing - we know there is supposed to be a nucleotide at that position (for some reason - e.g., to make an alignment), but we have no idea which one it is.

RFC4271 says the maximum BGP message size is 4096 bytes.

I was able to send a BGP message which has up to 4095 bytes using the latest code,

                                        raise Notify(6,0,'attributes size is so large we can not even pack one NLRI')

issue_title: exabgp startup failed
images.list() on Swarm always returns intermediate images even if called with 'all=False' (default)
issue_body:
images.list(all=True) and images.list(all=False) work as expected on a local Docker engine.

But if I execute the same commands against a Swarm cluster, I always get all images including intermediate ones, regardless of the value of the parameter 'all' (which should be set to False by default, by the way).

I create the client by loading Swarm env variables before starting Python and then using docker.from_env() statement.

I'm using the latest Docker version to date (client API 1.26, server API 1.25). Tried to change API version from 1.21 to 1.25 but the result is still the same: I cannot get the list of images without getting also the intermediate layers in the output.

The official docker client works as expected: I get the intermediate images only if I pass the '-a' switch to 'docker images'.

issue_title: [Question] Service healthcheck
issue_body:
Is there a way to specify a healthcheck command when [creating a new service](https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/services.html#docker.models.services.ServiceCollection.create) using the SDK?
This options is available in the docker cli using the flag ['--health-cmd'](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/service_create/#options) as well as in [container run](https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/containers.html#docker.models.containers.ContainerCollection.run) in the SDK but I couldn't find a way to specify healthcheck commands for services even in the low level API.

issue_title: Probably invalid version checking in container creation
issue_body:
Hi.
I've stumbled upon something i think is a not up-to-date code.

utils.py at line 794 in my distribution (1.6.0) states following:

    if compare_version('1.10', version) >= 0:
        message = ('{0!r} parameter has no effect on create_container().'
                   ' It has been moved to start()')
        if dns is not None:
            raise errors.InvalidVersion(message.format('dns'))
        if volumes_from is not None:
            raise errors.InvalidVersion(message.format('volumes_from'))

As far as i can read from docs, this is not true - both Dns and VolumesFrom are seen in HostConfig for API 1.21. Even if everything is okay with this code, there is still a problem i'm struggling to solve - some of the HostConfig options can be specified in `.container_create()`, but not on `.start()`, e.g. logging driver and options may be set only during creation, but if i need to use custom DNS, i have to specify it on `.start()` call, and this will override whole HostConfig, erasing my logging configuration. Since this code directly raise exceptions, i have no option but to create whole dict directly and pass it to `container_from_configuration()`.

I'm sorry i'm not doing direct pull request instead of issue, but i don't have enough time to figure out correct version constraints and get into projects conventions - sadly, i'm no pythonist and can't do it.

issue_title: Handler's rep is executed twice during encoding
issue_body:
One execution happens during `marshal` and another during `emit_encoded`. I think it is possible to reuse representation between the two. Test case:
```
import transit.writer
import StringIO

class rep(object):

    @staticmethod
    def tag(v):
        return 'tag'

    @staticmethod
    def rep(v):
        print 'rep'
        return []

class A(object):
    pass

w = transit.writer.Writer(StringIO.StringIO(), 'json')
w.register(A, rep)
w.write({'a': A()})
```
outputs:
```
rep
rep
```

issue_title: use requires.io for keeing requirements.txt up-to-date
issue_body:
https://requires.io/ is a great tool to keep dependencies up to date, while ensuring they have explictly pinned-down versions.
Once it's authorized (by someone with push access to the repo), it works like a bot that watches PyPI for new releases, and sends new pull requests to projects having some requirements.txt files, to update them files with the new requirement. The CI will then run the tests against the new version of that dependency, and the maintainers can merge the PR or fix any issues that may arise.

issue_title: running allennlp test-install after installing with pip leads to many errors
issue_body:
After installing AllenNLP with: `pip install git+git://github.com/allenai/allennlp.git` and running `allennlp test-install`, a bunch of errors come up. Raising an issue just to enumerate them all. A full gist of the test traceback is here: https://gist.github.com/nelson-liu/78d08ad0090ad8f95c34675942f5bb4d

- The biggest issue is that we lose the directory structure in the repo. For example, `allennlp` with pip would get installed in `/home/nfliu/miniconda3/envs/test/lib/python3.6/site-packages/allennlp`. this contains _only_ the module (so it's [this folder](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/tree/master/allennlp)).  The `tutorials`, `scripts`, `READMEs`, etc. aren't actually installed anywhere, so all of the paths in the tests break (e.g., script tests that shell out to run `perl ./scripts/some_eval_script.pl`).

- On a separate related note it looks like the precompiled custom extensions in the module aren't copied over either, maybe because they're not `.py` files and they get ignored automatically? This makes the `test_custom_extensions` / `alternating_lstm` tests break, even though they should work. This also means that custom extensions in general aren't working in the `pip install`ed version of allennlp.

## Things that are actually broken in the pip-installed version

- the custom highway lstm (since the extensions aren't included). fixed by #1229 
- `evalb_bracketing_scorer`, since `scripts/EVALB` does not exist. fixed by #1233 
- any tests that rely on files in `./tutorials` and `./scripts`. wip solution in #1232 

The other test errors are just errors with the tests themselves, not with the allennlp package. Which is unfortunate, but it doesn't affect _core_ library functionality.

issue_title: Saving and loading an in-memory `Model` & `config.json` is very hard
issue_body:
Here is a script I just wrote to load a model with the custom LSTM kernel and re-pack it into a new model. Turned out that we don't have any functionality for creating an archive from an in memory `Model` and `Config` and doing so was pretty messy. Hacking stuff like this is pretty key for e.g transfer learning and research in general - "what happens if I take part of this model and put it in this other one".


```
from allennlp.models.archival import load_archive, archive_model
from allennlp.common.params import Params
from allennlp.modules.seq2seq_encoders.seq2seq_encoder import Seq2SeqEncoder

import tarfile
import torch
import numpy as np
import os
import shutil
import json

root_dir = '/net/efs/aristo/allennlp/srl_lm/emnlp/srl/5b-elmo/'
fname = '/net/efs/aristo/allennlp/srl_lm/emnlp/srl/5b-elmo/model.tar.gz'

archive = load_archive(fname)


model = archive.model
params = archive.config
new_params = params.duplicate()

encoder_params = params.get("model").get("encoder").as_dict()
new_encoder_params = encoder_params
new_encoder_params["type"] = "alternating_lstm"
new_encoder_params["use_input_projection_bias"] = False
new_params["model"]["encoder"] = new_encoder_params

old_encoder = model.encoder._module
new_encoder = Seq2SeqEncoder.from_params(Params(new_encoder_params.copy()))


weight_index = 0
bias_index = 0

num_layers = 8
for layer_index in range(num_layers):
    layer = getattr(new_encoder._module, 'layer_%d' % layer_index)
    input_weight = layer.input_linearity.weight
    state_weight = layer.state_linearity.weight
    bias = layer.state_linearity.bias

    input_weight.data.t().copy_(old_encoder.weight.data[weight_index: weight_index + input_weight.nelement()].view_as(input_weight.t()))
    weight_index += input_weight.nelement()

    state_weight.data.t().copy_(old_encoder.weight.data[weight_index: weight_index + state_weight.nelement()].view_as(state_weight.t()))

    weight_index += state_weight.nelement()

    bias.data.copy_(old_encoder.bias.data[bias_index:bias_index + bias.nelement()])
    bias_index += bias.nelement()

model.encoder = new_encoder

serialisation_dir = "./new_model/"
os.makedirs("./new_model/")
print(serialisation_dir)
print(root_dir)
shutil.copytree(os.path.join(root_dir, "vocabulary"), os.path.join(serialisation_dir, "vocabulary"))

with open(os.path.join(root_dir, "files_to_archive.json"), "r") as f:
    fta = json.loads(f.read())

print(fta)
with open(os.path.join(serialisation_dir, "config.json"), "w") as f:
    f.write(json.dumps(new_params.as_dict(quiet=True)))

model_state = model.state_dict()
torch.save(model_state, os.path.join(serialisation_dir, "best.th"))

archive_model(serialisation_dir, files_to_archive=fta)
```

issue_title: Support pulling data from AWS S3 buckets directly (using boto3)
issue_body:
Sometimes we have to pull data files from a private S3 bucket, which requires AWS credentials, so we can't just access it with a simple HTTP GET request. To handle this, we could use `boto3` or the `awscli` to pull s3 files when the file is specified by an s3 path, like `s3://path-to-file`, instead of the http url. At this time it seems that the alternative is to just manually download the files locally first using the `awscli` or `boto3` and then point allennlp to the local copy.

The only downside I can see of supporting this feature is adding an additional dependency (`boto3`).

I'd be willing to submit a PR if this is something you want to support.

issue_title: Postgres password required in diamond config file
issue_body:
The postgres collector has a `postgres` default string for the password connection. This basically forces to override the password by writing it in clear text in the `diamond.conf` file or to use a weak auth mode such as  `trust` in `pg_hba.conf`.

There should be a way to not pass a password to the psycopg2 `connect()` function so that the password can be specified in the `.pgpass` file.

issue_title: pytest-virtualenv: Python version agnostic virtualenv executable
issue_body:
Hi,
in `pytest-virtualenv` the `VIRTUALENV_FIXTURE_EXECUTABLE` detects some version of the virtualenv executable, but not necessarily the one running on the correct Python 2 or Python 3 version.

I.e. in a Python 3 script on Fedora, the executable detected is `/usr/bin/virtualenv` that runs on Python 2 (Python 3 version is at `/usr/bin/virtualenv-3.6`).

How about changing the executable definition to `sys.executable + "-m virtualenv"`—i.e. `/usr/bin/python3 -m virtualenv` for Python 3?

issue_title: Added "current index" support for projections
issue_body:
It is sometimes useful when in a projection to be able to know which element of the thing being projected you are evaluating. When projecting on an array, the index would be the current index of the array (starting at 0), and when projecting an object, the index would be the current key of the array. I would suggest adding a new token to the grammar to represent the "current_node". The character I would suggest is `#`.

I can flesh this out with more details and a JEP if there's interest in something like this.

issue_title: get-identities: remove --usernames & --identities
issue_body:
Why does "globus get-identities" need the --usernames and --identities options?  They have unambiguous syntax.  A username is always user@domain, and an identity id is always a UUID.  The CLI should just figure that out, and not make me add the option.

Note that "globus bookmark show" does essentially the same thing, allowing me to input either a bookmark name or id, but it does not require me to include an option to say which I am inputting.

issue_title: Add `--filter` option to `globus ls`
issue_body:
We should expose the `--filter` parameter for `ls`, as it will give users much richer capabilities including glob-like functionality.

Reference for the API's filter param: https://docs.globus.org/api/transfer/file_operations/#list_directory_contents

I think it may be best to simply expose this parameter as a single option which directly passes values through. That probably means that we need to replicate the documentation for `filter` from the API, in the form of CLI doc.
Also, I think an example of simulating `ls | grep` with `globus ls --filter` would be nice.

issue_title: Fix `globus update` invocation of `pip` to work on pip>=9.0.2
issue_body:
Okay, it turns out that we've been using `pip.main` because I mistakenly believed that this was a public, supported API.

It turns out not to be the case, and this is not supported usage.
It breaks on pip 9.0.2 and pip 10.x (beta now available) and we need to fix.

Full doc on what to do:
https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/user_guide/#using-pip-from-your-program

`pip` issue with the most detail and gnashing of teeth:
https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/5081

---

I think it would be lovely if `pip` itself implemented the `subprocess` logic, but I don't want to wait for us to fix this and start getting it out.
I'll put together and test a fix now, and then we can rebase #392 to use the changed version.

issue_title: Minimal testnet for sharding
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

We need the minimal P2P code to do the following.

- Maintain connections to peers, probably from a hardcoded list or at least a list of bootnodes.
- Gossip protocol to pass around new blocks/collations.
- ???

### How can it be fixed

Starting point should probably be

- `p2p.lightchain.LightChain` 
- `p2p.peer.LESPeer`
- `p2p.les`

We'll need to implement `Command` classes for a minimal subset of commands needed to pass around blocks.  Looking at the existing commands we will probably need a set of commands loosely as follows.

- `Status` - For initial handshake and connecting to *useful* peers.
- `Announce` - For letting peers know they have a new block.
- `GetXXX` and `XXX` for things like requesting and sending blocks or headers.

Then we'll need to implement a `Protocol` class to wrap these commands, similar to `LESProtocol`.

And then finally, a `ShardChain` class like `LightChain` which uses the protocol to join the network, find suitable peers, learn the appropraite chain of blocks that it should sync with and then request the blocks and headers and do whatever is needed with them.

issue_title: Empty sequences for configuration parameters cannot be expressed in environment variables
issue_body:
<!--
Hi!

This is an issue tracker for conda -- the package manager.  File feature requests
for conda here, as well as bug reports about something conda has messed up.

If your issue is a bug report or feature request for:
  * a specific conda package from Anaconda ('defaults' channel):
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * a specific conda package from conda-forge:
      ==> file at the corresponding feedstock under https://github.com/conda-forge
  * repo.continuum.io access and service:
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * anaconda.org access and service:
      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report
  * commands under 'conda build':
      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build
  * commands under 'conda env':
      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

-->

**I'm submitting a...**
  - [ ] bug report
  - [x] feature request


### Current Behavior
<!-- What actually happens? -->
For a configuration parameter that accepts sequences, an empty value in the corresponding environment variable gets interpreted as an one-element sequence containing an empty string, i.e., `[""]`. Thus there is no way to specify an empty sequence via an environment variable.

### Steps to Reproduce
<!-- If the current behavior is a bug, please provide specific, minimal steps to independently reproduce.
     Include the exact conda commands that reproduce the issue. -->
```bash
$ export CONDA_CHANNELS=
$ conda config --show channels
channels:
  - 
$ export CONDA_CHANNELS=defaults,
$ conda config --show channels
channels:
  - defaults
  - 
$ export CONDA_CHANNELS=defaults,,
$ conda config --show channels
channels:
  - defaults
  - 
```

### Expected Behavior
<!-- What do you think should happen? -->
One possibility to allow empty sequences would be to allow and ignore trailing separator, i.e., interpret
  * `,` as `[]`,
  *  `,,` as `[""]`,
  * `,,,` as `["", ""]`,

channels:

issue_title: Enhance conda's interoperability with pip
issue_body:
> As a conda user who also uses pip on the same environment, I want conda to be smart about interacting with what pip has installed, so that I don't have clobbering, double installs, and other bad interactions.

- [ ] Remove dist from conda/resolve.py https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/6979
- [ ] Remove dist from remainder of project.
- [ ] Establish preliminary framework for shadow packages.
- [ ] Add pip shadow packages into PrefixData
- [ ] Teach conda/resolve.py to freeze immutable pip shadow packages 
- [ ] Teach conda/resolve.py to be able to update mutable pip shadow packages
- [ ] Teach UnlinkLinkTransaction to uninstall mutable pip shadow packages

issue_title: Replace /bin/bash with /usr/bin/env bash
issue_body:
Currently `conda` hardcodes `#!/bin/bash`. If instead `#!/usr/bin/env bash` would be used, then the version of the current environment would be used.

I'm attempting to package `conda` at the moment for an OS where `#!/bin/bash` is unavailable, but `#!/usr/bin/env bash` does work.

The same by the way applies to some of the packages offered via `conda`.

issue_title: git compile fails when curl is installed
issue_body:
I just discovered this obscure bug that I'm unable to compile git (I don't have root on some of my Linux servers, that's why I do that) when the `curl` package is installed, due to some C_PATH problem caused by that:
```bash
imap-send.c: In function ‘setup_curl’:
imap-send.c:1428: error: ‘CURLOPT_LOGIN_OPTIONS’ undeclared (first use in this function)
imap-send.c:1428: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once
imap-send.c:1428: error: for each function it appears in.)
imap-send.c:1428: warning: type defaults to ‘int’ in declaration of ‘_curl_opt’
make: *** [imap-send.o] Error 1
```

This is probably explained by this:
http://git.661346.n2.nabble.com/Compiling-2-4-0-doesn-t-seem-to-honour-C-INCLUDE-PATH-td7629976.html

and/or by the harshly different versions that exist on this system?:
```bash
klay6683 at maven-iuvs-itf in ~/src/iuvs (master●●)
$ /usr/bin/curl --version
curl 7.19.7 (x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu) libcurl/7.19.7 NSS/3.14.3.0 zlib/1.2.3 libidn/1.18 libssh2/1.4.2
Protocols: tftp ftp telnet dict ldap ldaps http file https ftps scp sftp
Features: GSS-Negotiate IDN IPv6 Largefile NTLM SSL libz

klay6683 at maven-iuvs-itf in ~/src/iuvs (master●●)
$ curl --version
curl 7.43.0 (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu) libcurl/7.43.0 OpenSSL/1.0.1k zlib/1.2.8
Protocols: dict file ftp ftps gopher http https imap imaps pop3 pop3s rtsp smb smbs smtp smtps telnet tftp
Features: IPv6 Largefile GSS-API Kerberos SPNEGO NTLM NTLM_WB SSL libz TLS-SRP UnixSockets
```

Anyhow, this can be avoided by creating an environment that does **NOT** have `curl` installed (confirmed).
Can this be somehow avoided for the case when the `curl` package is installed?

issue_title: Can't install rstudio from Anaconda navigator:  specifications in conflict: rstudio ==1.0.136
issue_body:
I just tried installing rstudio from Anaconda navigator but it failed due to conflicting specifications.

Here's exactly what happened:

- In Anaconda navigator I pressed the "Install" button under the rstudio icon. (Under the icon version number 1.0.136 was mentioned.)
- After a while, I got a prompt telling me to set up a new environment for Python 3.5/3.6:
![capture](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/28363225/25678603/20b824d6-2fff-11e7-8159-6917360e244d.PNG)
- I pressed "Create".
- After waiting a little longer, I got the following "Conda process error":
```
The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
- rstudio ==1.0.136
Use "conda info " to see the dependencies for each package.
```

The same thing happened when trying to install rstudio 1.0.44 via the gear icon, except that it said that `rstudio ==1.0.44` was in conflict.

I also tried installing via Anaconda prompt and got a similar error. (It doesn't look like Anaconda tried to fix the Python 2/3 problem in this case.):
```
(C:\Python27\Anaconda2) C:\Users\Joey>conda install rstudio
Fetching package metadata .............
Solving package specifications: .


UnsatisfiableError: The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - rstudio
Use "conda info <package>" to see the dependencies for each package.
```

issue_title: Run solver for explicit installs by default
issue_body:
Currently, explicit installs, i.e. `conda install /path/to/conda-package.tar.bz2` install only that package, and do not invoke the solver.  This has been the behavior since the feature was first implemented, likely driven by implementation simplicity.

The generalized correct flow for a generic conda install should be:

1. Download/extract step for explicit conda packages (and wheels, if capability is added)
2. Invoke solver
3. Download/extract step for packages as indicated by the solver
4. Unlink/link transaction

In some situations, this will be a breaking change in behavior for users, so we'll have to wait for a feature release to incorporate this change.  We'll also want to add an optional cli flag, perhaps `--explicit` or `--no-solve`, to let conda return to the previous behavior for `create` and `install` commands.

This change will fix several old issues, where if a package already exists in an environment, and then a user does an explicit install, the old package isn't removed from the environment (paths are generally just clobbered) and conda also thinks two versions of the package is installed in the environment because the old `conda-meta` file isn't removed.

An implementation here will also potentially touch the implementation of `conda create --clone`.

issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:

```
root@922395dccc83:~# sudo -u jenkins /opt/conda/bin/conda install -q -y conda


Package plan for installation in environment /opt/conda:

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    conda:   4.3.31-py36_0        --> 4.4.3-py36_0        
    pycosat: 0.6.2-py36h1a0ea17_1 --> 0.6.3-py36h0a5515d_0

root@922395dccc83:~# sudo -u jenkins /opt/conda/bin/conda install -q -y numpy
Solving environment: ...working... failed

NotWritableError: The current user does not have write permissions to a required path.
  path: /root/.conda/pkgs/urls.txt
  uid: 1014
  gid: 1014

If you feel that permissions on this path are set incorrectly, you can manually
change them by executing

  $ sudo chown 1014:1014 /root/.conda/pkgs/urls.txt

```

In older versions of Conda, it would not attempt to write to HOME when installing packages.

The end user here can easily solve the problem by passing `-H` to `sudo` (thus causing HOME to be set correctly in this case).

issue_title: `conda update conda` + process crash = unsuable conda installation
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior Conda left in a non-working state after crash while `conda update conda`. ### Steps to Reproduce The steps below might not always reproduce the bug. Please see additional information below. As no-admin user: ``` conda update conda ``` Kill process while running. Since I was running under a non-admin user, it run into a bug of conda spawning hundreds of `pythonw.exe` processes in a deadlock, similar to issue #4782. A Ctrl+C didn't work so I had to kill the process window. After that, `conda` command was not found in the system. After investigating, all conda related files under `Scripts`and `Lib/site-packages/conda` and `Lib/site-packages/conda-ev` were renamed to include an extra extension `.c~`. ## Expected Behavior Even though the root cause was another bug, and the process was killed on the run, the likelihood of leaving the system in an unsusable state should be minimized, or at least a recovery script added before starting changing the original files. ## Potential Solution A script to rename all files back to their original extension (`.exe.c~ -> .exe` and `.py.c~ -> .py`) did bring conda came back to live nicely. ``` import os import sys import glob def doit(folder): os.chdir(folder) files = glob.glob("*.c~") print(folder) print('Files found:') print(files) all = False for f in files: if os.path.exists(f[:-3]): if not all: print('File %s exists - replace it? [yna]' % f[:-3]) ans = input() if ans.lower() == 'n': continue elif ans.lower() == 'a': all = True os.remove(f[:-3]) os.rename(f, f[:-3]) if __name__ == '__main__': dirs = ['C:\\Anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\conda_env\\', 'C:\\Anaconda3\\Lib\\site-packages\\conda\\', 'C:\\Anaconda3\\Scripts\\',] for topdir in dirs: for root, dirs, files in os.walk(topdir): doit(root) ``` ## Environment Information

issue_title: Colon used as separator
issue_body: Would you consider allowing either the colon or the equal sign as a separator as ConfigParser supports?

issue_title: Support optional sections during validation
issue_body: Optional key & values are already supported by custom validators, but there is not currently a syntax for optional sections. I suggest supporting this feature via a keyword similar to `[__many__]`, perhaps `[__optional__foo]` or `[__optional__][foo]`.

issue_title: Exceptions on sniffing error
issue_body:
After 2d82b1b1739ba92f4de6c3f2eb88bcac9563bc70 a next call to ElasticSearch server may raise an error if the last sniffing was failed.

As I see `elasticsearch-py` suppresses exceptions like `ConnectionError` but `elasticsearch-py-async` raises all errors (connections, timeouts, cancellations etc).
`elasticsearch-py` raises `TransportError` only as I see.

Another problem is when the library reports about problems: `elasticsearch-py` raises immediatelly but `elasticsearch-py-async` will report about problem in sniffing in the past.

I did not dig into the code too deep but maybe moving sniffing from synchronous `get_connection` into async `perform_request` makes sense?

issue_title: Unable to upload large files
issue_body:
I tried to use blobxfer to upload a large file (around 264 GB) to an azure block blob storage. The tool could not upload the file. The error message was:

> RuntimeError: 63084 chunks for file my_big_file exceeds Azure Storage limits for a single block blob

I believe that this is due to the limitation on the MAX_BLOB_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES set to 4194304 (63084 * 4194304 B = 264 GB). According to [azure documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-subscription-service-limits#storage-limits), the maximum number of blocks in a block blob is 50000 blocks of 100 MB. 63084 blocks is of course over the limit for the number of blocks and that is why it fails. But I don't understand this artificial limitation to the maximum block size of 4194304 bytes (which is 4 MB) imposed by blobxfer, which is 25 times less than the maximum in the azure documentation.

Anyway, the quick fix for me was to increase the value of MAX_BLOB_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES to a higher value and then I could upload my file without any issue.

issue_title: [Versioning] Mechanism for importing versioned models is too cumbersome
issue_body:
From PR #2526:

Original
```Python
from azure.mgmt.storage.models import Encryption, EncryptionServices, EncryptionService
```

Current
```Python
Encryption, EncryptionServices, EncryptionService = versioned_models(ResourceType.MGMT_STORAGE_STORAGE_ACCOUNTS, 'Encryption', 'EncryptionServices', 'EncryptionService')
```

Before we apply the second pattern wholesale across the CLI, we should investigate how we can approach the ideal of the original, handling the versioning behind this scenes.

issue_title: [Backup] Outstanding issues/enhancements prior to GA
issue_body:
Items which should be resolved prior to GA:
- [ ] Finalize command tree structure to support multiple types of backup loads.
- [ ] Support `--ids` 
- [ ] "name" parameters should accept the name format as output from the show command (for example: for `--container-name` it is fine to accept the VM name as a shorthand, but it should definitely accept the actual container name (`IaasVMContainer;iaasvmcontainerv2;{rg};{vm}`).
- [ ] Add examples to commands whose usage is unclear.
- [ ] Add completer support for named parameters to enable TAB completion.
- [ ] Add logic to choose an appropriate default location for `vault create`.
- [ ] Any update commands must support the CLI's generic update pattern.
- [ ] Add defaulting logic to the protection enable command for the --profile parameter.

issue_title: data lake cli (dls) upload / download chunk and block size support
issue_body:
### Description
The default chunk size (268MB) prevents parallelization of smaller transfers.
Exposing additional parameters (chunk, buffer, block size) in the CLI would allow to take advantage of parallel transfers for smaller files.

---

```



- name: --chunk-size
  | type: int
  | short-summary: 'Number of bytes for a chunk. Large files are split into chunks. Files smaller than this number will always be transferred in a single thread. Default 268435456'
  | - name: --buffer-size
  | type: int
  | short-summary: 'Number of bytes for internal buffer. This block cannot be bigger than a chunk and cannot be smaller than a block. Default 4194304'
  | - name: --block-size
  | type: int
  | short-summary: 'Number of bytes for a block. Within each chunk, we write a smaller block for each API call. This block cannot be bigger than a chunk. Default:4194304'
 
```

issue_title: Create a PR checklist to ensure completeness.
issue_body:
It would be nice to have a PR checklist for submitters in a similar vein to the checklist for Azure PowerShell (e.g., https://github.com/Azure/azure-powershell/pull/3466).

As a starting point, I would suggest, at a minimum:
* General:
    * Contribution guidelines.
* Tests:
    * Links to test information.
    * At least one unit test per command.
    * At least two popular scenario tests for each command.
* Documentation:
    * A meaningful description for each command added or updated.
    * A meaningful description for each parameter added or updated.
    * At least one meaningful example for each command added or updated.
* Anything else we think is a good idea to steal from Azure PowerShell.

issue_title: CI Version check script can allow changes to be logged under the wrong version
issue_body:
Because there is approx. a week between code complete and the public release, there exists about a week gap where merged PRs could inadvertently log their changes under the already cut release instead of under a new version for the subsequent release and the CI would not catch this.

For example:

1. CLI is code-complete and build released to Cloud Shell
2. The next PR for network inadvertently puts its changes under the existing version that has already been released to Cloud Shell. The CI will not fail.
3. On the release day, the packages are published to PyPI. 
4. A new PR is created for network and the CI complains. Only then is a new version added to HISTORY, but the PR that was merged earlier (which should logged under the new version) will still be listed in the old version.

---

issue_title: Subcommand for returning a valid accessToken
issue_body:
`gcloud config config-helper` returns a JSON blob with a number of things about the current configuration of the CLI, along with an properly-refreshed, active token for applications to use. This is a feature request for a similar functionality in the `az` CLI.

Scenario: In Kubernetes, we are trying to emulate the Google Auth plugin which uses `gcloud config config-helper` to get a refreshed accessToken and then uses it for authentication in it's own ways. Currently, the Azure implementation reaches into `~/.azure/` directly to read the access token. It would be much better if this was done via os.Exec'ing `az config config-helper` or something similar, so that the CLI can refresh the token if needed and then return it.

Can we create a command like `config config-helper` in `az`?

cc: @brendandburns

issue_title: [Discussion] Typing a command group should print its help text by default
issue_body:
It would be great if typing a command group (e.g. `az component`, `az network`) would print out the same text that you get when appending the `-h` flag. This would make the already well-documented nature of the CLI feel a little easier to navigate. For example, typing `az network` does in fact print out the list of acceptable commands, but it's a pretty long list, with no help descriptions, and so you really have to add `-h` to make that text usable.

I've always felt like help flags were mostly used for commands (to indicate that you don't want to execute it), and not command groups, and many CLIs special-case "executing" a command group by printing out its pretty-printed help text. For example, if you run `docker network` (or any other group/sub group), you get the full help text, that is analogous to what you'd get by running `az network -h`. It's a subtle difference, but saving a few keystrokes can go a long way when using a CLI a lot :)

This behavior would seem conceptually similar to the fact that simply typing `az` (which is the "root" command group) outputs the print-printed help text for the top-level command groups.

issue_title: Manifest not being applied until button in portal is clicked
InsecurePlatformWarning on python 2.7.9 or less
issue_body:
On Python 2.7.9 or less, we get the follow warning message.

You will see this message during install and also when you run `az login`.

InsecurePlatformWarning
```
/usr/local/az/envs/default/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/packages/urllib3/util/ssl_.py:122: InsecurePlatformWarning: A true SSLContext object is not available. This prevents urllib3 from configuring SSL appropriately and may cause certain SSL connections to fail. You can upgrade to a newer version of Python to solve this. For more information, see https://urllib3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/security.html#insecureplatformwarning.
  InsecurePlatformWarning
```

Current Workaround:
Tell the user to use pip to install the required dependencies.
e.g. `sudo /usr/local/az/envs/default/bin/pip install pyopenssl ndg-httpsclient pyasn1`

STEPS TO REPRODUCE:
Create a Ubuntu 14.04 LTS VM and install the CLI.
You will see the warnings during install and also when you run `az login`.

SUGGESTED SOLUTION:
In `setup.py` of azure-cli package, we should optionally install pyopenssl, ndg-httpsclient and pyasn1 if python version is less that 2.7.9.


References:
- http://stackoverflow.com/a/29099439
- http://stackoverflow.com/a/29202163

issue_title: Error masked by a JSON decoder error
issue_body:
In KustoClient in the `_execute` method, when the `response.status_code` is not equal to `200`, the code [throws an exception](https://github.com/Azure/azure-kusto-python/blob/master/azure-kusto-data/azure/kusto/data/kusto_client.py#L356) converting the response to json format. However in cases where the response is not convertible to json, the original error is masked and a `JSONDecodeError` is thrown.

This should be fixed in such a way that the original error is not masked so that the user can fix their problem.

I have encountered this problem when my application didn't have access to the Kusto cluster. The original response status code was 403.

issue_title: create_blob_from_path does not set blob content MD5 if file is >64MB
issue_body:
Issue: create_blob_from_path does not set the blob content MD5 if file is >64MB. The equivalent Node function (createBlockBlobFromLocalFile) does.

Impact : Uploading a file from older Azure Xplat CLI will set blob content MD5 but the new one Azure CLI 2.0 does not, causing a regression.

The CLI 2.0 call
https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/2ea1eb3fb9a21b7dcc546d5e1c5859329275be5f/src/command_modules/azure-cli-storage/azure/cli/command_modules/storage/blob.py#L217

Python SDK implementation of the create_blob_from_path call
https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/blob/9b5e870e615dca75cf9edcf7d68b2cc5f1a61e02/azure-storage-blob/azure/storage/blob/blockblobservice.py#L325

Tracing shows that the final **Put Block List** call does not carry the **x-ms-blob-content-md5** header but the NodeJS SDK does.

NodeJS implementation of createBlockBlobFromLocalFile
https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-node/blob/8f6fe1fd542bc7200f883b9cf296b2fc284356b5/lib/services/blob/blobservice.node.js#L177

issue_title: 900 MB CSV file = 18 GB of RAM, fails to complete (GNAF)
issue_body:
Trying to process [the new Australian GNAF source](https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/blob/au-gnaf/sources/au/countrywide.json) is failing on the production machines. I ran it manually via `openaddr-process-one` on my development machine and suspect it's related to memory usage. I tested with python2 on 64 bit Ubuntu with the default install options.

The source is a 900MB CSV file. The process job takes up to 18.2GB of virtual memory and as much resident as it can get. My development machine has 16GB of RAM and 16GB of swap. Running the job it quickly ballooned up to 13GB resident size. And it seemed to keep swapping, so it's actively touching that memory. There's some oddness in memory usage I don't fully understand, but it's definitely Too Much.

I gave up running it after about an hour, in which the process only used 7 minutes of actual CPU time. Presumably the rest of the time went to swapping. The `converted` directory inside the `process_one` temporary directory had no files in it, which suggests it never got to actually writing any output. (*edit*: my mistake, there is an intermediate output file written in `/tmp`.)

It's been a year since I looked at the code, but my memory was that CSV sources processed in constant memory of like 100MB, nothing proportional to input size. I think this memory use needs more investigation.

issue_title: Support for commit-msg
issue_body:
Totally not trying to be snarky here-- the project is called _pre-commit_, after all-- but is there a reason commit-msg hooks are not supported? 

Being able to validate commit messages for appropriate issue-tracking ids, length, etc is important for a lot of us and having to put together another mechanism to deal with it or utilize another framework alongside pre-commit seems non-DRY-y.

I would put this as a "pro" for overcommit (see #384) but I'd really like to see it in pre-commit, as overcommit's performance in Windows is... subpar. (More of a ruby issue than overcommit.)

issue_title: Error fetching results when user provides a COS bucket prefix
issue_body:
When a user provides a prefix and submits an SQL query, it's `resultset_location` is something like:

```
cos://s3-api.us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net/sqlresultsbucket/my_prefix/jobid=93d643f5-26a9-452f-8eae-de4e17f88032
```

However, when it tries to get the results of the query, it's currently looks for something like:

```
https://s3-api.us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net/sqlresultsbucket?prefix=my_prefixjobid=93d643f5-26a9-452f-8eae-de4e17f88032/part
```

It will not find the data since it's missing the `/` between the prefix and the `jobid`. Therefore, there needs to be a check in place to see whether there is a prefix and if there is, then add on `/` before fetching the results.

issue_title: Make grading plots less horrible
issue_body:
Grading plots is a huge pain. Partially, they just need to be done manually, but there are also some things that could be autograded. However, if the plots are done with matplotlib at least, this is difficult, as the underlying data model doesn't necessarily correspond one-to-one with what the plot looks like. For example:

1. `plot` and `scatter` can produce nearly identical plots, but one is accessed through lines and one is accessed through collections.
2. It's possible to use `scatter` within a for loop for just a single point but with an array of colors, meaning that when we check the point colors they seem correct, but they're actually not because the points all just use the first color of the color array.
3. How to check things like a dendrogram plot?

This might not actually be something nbgrader wants to handle, especially if it wants to be language agnostic, but I'd really like a better way to do this so I'm opening an issue here for now anyway.

issue_title: Add a warning/error for code cells that are marked as both grade cells and solution cells
issue_body: The only cells that are marked as both grade and solution should be cells that need to be graded manually, e.g. markdown cells. I can see some use cases for doing this with code cells as well, so maybe there shouldn't be an actual error, but at least a warning in `nbgrader assign` because probably in both cases if a code cell is both a grade cell and a solution cell, it's a mistake.

issue_body:

the dockerfile runs the jupyter notebook with root privileges. The [docker security documentation](https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/security/) states:

The possibility to run arbitrary code as root via http is a concern even in a cgroup environment.

issue_title: Adopt pandas-style Jupyter contribution guide
issue_body:
During the November 24th developer meeting, there was a discussion around the friendliness and accessibility of our documentation, especially with regard to new contributors.

I'd like to propose that we create a new Jupyter Contribution Guide that goes a step beyond the previous [IPython Contribution Guide](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) that we link to in all of the repositories and incorporates some of the great additions @jhamrick made to the contribution guide.

I selected `pandas` as a model for a contribution guide because [their guide](https://github.com/pydata/pandas/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) was immensely helpful to me when I submitted my first ever PR to an OS project. We can incorporate features of other contribution guidelines into this, of course.

It might be a good idea (and very meta) to have a first time contributor submit a PR for this with the help of one of the more regular developers just so we can make sure the guidelines have the right perspective.

Thoughts?

cc/ @ellisonbg @fperez @willingc

issue_body:
OS X 10.9.5

Python `3.5.0`

`jupyter` version `1.0.0`

`notebook` version `4.0.6`

`ipython` version `4.0.1` (in case that's relevant).

Lately, whenever I've tried `jupyter notebook` or `ipython notebook`, I got the following stack trace, which is always spat out three times in the terminal:

```
[W 14:37:24.649 NotebookApp] Error loading server extension urth.widgets.ext.urth_import

      File "/Users/[username]/.pyenv/versions/stochastic-order/lib/python3.5/site-packages/notebook/notebookapp.py", line 991, in init_server_extensions

      File "/Users/[username]/.pyenv/versions/3.5.0/lib/python3.5/importlib/__init__.py", line 126, in import_module

      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 986, in _gcd_import
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 969, in _find_and_load
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 944, in _find_and_load_unlocked
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 222, in _call_with_frames_removed
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 986, in _gcd_import
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 969, in _find_and_load
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 944, in _find_and_load_unlocked
      File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 222, in _call_with_frames_removed

issue_title: Add an option to make full use of browser window width
issue_body:
When using the notebook in a full browser window, all the cells and code easily fit inside the window and there is no need to scroll a cell to the side to view a long line of code. However, I often use the notebook in browser opened side by side with another window on the same screen. And when I do this, some cells with long lines of code (usually still shorter than 80 characters) become scroll-able to the side although the cells could take all the browser window width. It would be much easier if there is an option to extend the cells to take the full width of the browser window. I think this would be a very useful feature.

Thanks.

issue_title: Shortcut help for 'split cell' shortcut - display broken
issue_body:
![screenshot from 2016-01-27 11 05 50](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/327925/12611708/19856f96-c4e6-11e5-98f7-e0d800cf59a0.png)

I would never call that key 'subtract', either. I'd normally say 'dash', and I'd consider 'hyphen' or 'minus' valid alternatives ('minus' is the symbol, 'subtract' is the operation it indicates). The Unicode character it inserts is called HYPHEN-MINUS.

Also, I don't think the shortcut should be duplicated in help. We should pick one way to represent it.

issue_title: Role of words "mulled" and "biocontainers"
issue_body:
I'm trying to decide how to think about mulled and biocontainers and about how to expose it to planemo users. In my head - "mulled" is a build tool that builds "biocontainers". I'm not sure it is worth the cognitive load to expose both to tool developers however. For instance, should

```
$ planemo test --mulled_containers <tool>
```

be

```
$ planemo test --biocontainers <tool>
```

And should

```
$ planemo mull <tool>
```

be something like

```
$ planemo biocontainer_build <tool>
```

Ping @bgruening in particular but I'd like to hear advice from someone more removed as well.

issue_title: Switch from scrypt to cryptography's Scrypt back-end
issue_body:
In the upcoming release, we use `scrypt` for key-lengthening in the wallet implementation.  We might be able to get away with using the `scrypt` backend in the cryptography.io package ([docs](https://cryptography.io/en/latest/hazmat/backends/interfaces/#cryptography.hazmat.backends.interfaces.ScryptBackend.derive_scrypt)).  This would be nice since it cuts down our dependency graph and it makes the Mac OS X build easier to come by.

Thoughts @kantai?

issue_title: Wallet integration with Portal
issue_body:
* Supporting wallet entirely on portal *

To support using portal's wallet for both identity and coin storage, core needs to support an API endpoint for generating transaction templates that can be signed and sent by portal.
See issue #363 

* Supporting core wallet via the API *

If we move forward with using the core wallet instead of the portal wallet, we will need an API that lets users work with wallets. They need to be able to create, delete, backup, and restore wallets.

Importantly, these methods should require the wallet password as a parameter _not_ the API key.

Currently, the API server requires the wallet password on startup. The API server should be able to start without yet knowing the wallet password, and fail on endpoints that require the password if it isn't entered yet.

issue_title: Pynsq fails to send large messages when using Deflate / Snappy compression
issue_body:
## The Problem:
During sending relatively big messages with one of the specified compression algorithms (deflate, snappy) server responds with Error (unexpected EOF).

## The cause:
When compression type is specified the connection is being upgraded with custom Socket classes, which have own `send` methods. In those send methods original socket had one attempt to send the whole pack of compressed data without validation of integrity. The custom `send` method returns the original length of uncompressed data despite the actual amount of sent bytes.
https://github.com/nsqio/pynsq/blob/v0.8.2/nsq/snappy_socket.py#L43
https://github.com/nsqio/pynsq/blob/v0.8.2/nsq/deflate_socket.py#L44

## Solution:
Remove compress-on-flight inside the `send` method - replace it with the original `socket.send`.
Add Encoder's classes which will compress with specified algorithms **before** sending data through the socket / `tornado.IOstream`.

issue_title: default CLI args not over-written by config settings
issue_body:
I have a config file that contains
```[server]
port=7000
```
However, the server still uses the default port value (5696).  I see that is because
1. the OptionParser has the "port" option using a default of 5696. This causes KmipServer._setup_configuration() to have its 'port' parameter set to 5696
2. KmipServer._setup_configuration() calls self.config.load_settings() *before* handling the settings passed in its input arguments.

Fixing this may be as simple as moving the call to config.load_settings() down a few lines (after the handling of the input parameters).

issue_title: Setup CI for rna-seq CGL
Allow for concurrent execution of RNASeq tests

Currently the output of the RNASeq tests are hard-coded. If two or more builds are executing the same test concurrently—and this is not an unlikely scenario when building PRs on Jenkins—they will overwrite each other's output.

Te output directory in S3 should be chosen pseudo-randomly and a clean way would be to set output_dir in setupClass(). Unfortunately, the tests currently all are flat functions so we may have to convert them to methods of a unittest.TestCase subclass. The alternative is passing input_dir and output_dir around as parameters.

issue_title: Spark/HDFS leader can die, causing our cluster to discover the true depths of sadness
issue_body:
See #123. Essentially, since we bid spot instances for all of the instances in our cluster, the Spark master and HDFS namenode run on a spot instance. If and when this instance is revoked, our cluster achieves a deepness of despair that is truly bleak. Our worker nodes fail, but cannot be recovered since our Spark/HDFS workers and Spark jobs rely on knowing the master IP via parameters, and we cannot meaningfully restart them (pointed at a new master) without modifying the jobstore.

To fix this, we have worked out a modest proposal:

* @hannes-ucsc will add hooks to the überscript that identify when a cluster has lost a node, and kills all of the nodes in a cluster. This is crude, but suffices.
* @hannes-ucsc will update cgcloud so that we can tag instances (e.g., the master), which makes it easy to dynamically find the master IP.
* @fnothaft will restructure the `adam_pipeline` to resolve some other issues (job decomposition) that make it impossible to reliably restart the ADAM jobs due to lack of input/output checkpointing

Or, just run on-demand instances.

issue_title: If method process fails, the Exception is ignored
issue_body:
In https://github.com/androguard/androguard/blob/master/androguard/decompiler/dad/decompile.py#L261 the exception of the processed method is caught and never raised.
This causes problems, if some error occurs while decompiling. Then the resulting source will be broken.

It really depends on the analysis method, what you want in this situation.
For me, where I mostly dump the whole source, it is certainly good to know if an exception happens, so I can use other decompilation techniques.

I'm not really sure how to tackle that problem.
In my case, I "fixed" it by raising the exception and try other methods then, if the get_source() function of a Class fails.
I'm also not sure if it is wise to implement too many flags, like a nonstopmode, a raiseexception mode, ... and whatnotelse.

issue_title: Attempting to send '--cov-report' to coveralls
issue_body:
The pytest-cov [documentation](http://pytest-cov.readthedocs.io/en/latest/readme.html#reporting) states the following:

>These three report options output to files without showing anything on the terminal:
>
>```python
>    py.test --cov-report html
>            --cov-report xml
>            --cov-report annotate
>            --cov=myproj tests/
>```
>
>The output location for each of these reports can be specified. The output location for the XML report is
>a file. Where as the output location for the HTML and annotated source code reports are directories:
>
>```python
>py.test --cov-report html:cov_html
>         --cov-report xml:cov.xml
>         --cov-report annotate:cov_annotate
>         --cov=myproj tests/
>```
>
>The final report option can also suppress printing to the terminal:
>
>```python
>py.test --cov-report= --cov=myproj tests/
>```
>
>This mode can be especially useful on continuous integration servers, where a coverage file is needed 
>for subsequent processing, but no local report needs to be viewed. For example, tests run on Travis-CI 
>could produce a .coverage file for use with Coveralls.

But, how do I send the redirected report generated from `--cov-report`, to coveralls?  I am currently using this framework within a series of docker containers, which my `.travis.yml` spins up for unit testing.  This framework generates the coverage report, and copied from a docker container, to the host for the Travis CI.  Then, my implementation of [python-coveralls](https://github.com/z4r/python-coveralls/issues/59), used explicitly by Travis is responsible for sending the coverage report to coveralls:

```python
        pytest.main([
            '--cov', '.',
            '--cov-report', 'xml:/var/machine-learning/coverage.xml',
            'test/live_server'
        ])
```

However, it seems the xml option is an [invalid format](https://travis-ci.org/jeff1evesque/machine-learning/builds/192214255#L6751-L6763) for sending to coveralls.

**Note:** this is the associated [issue](https://github.com/jeff1evesque/machine-learning/issues/2697) I'm working on corresponding to the above statement.

issue_title: xdist misidentifies failing test on teardown crash
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm seeing xdist flagging the wrong test when a crash happens at test teardown.
Given 2 tests:
- `test_a`, passes but crashes at teardown
- `test_b`, passes

xdist will identify `test_b` as failing.
I have been able to reproduce while using pytest-xdist and pytest-timeout.
Here are the steps I've been following:
- Test file:
```
import pytest
import time

@pytest.fixture
def timeout_fixture(request):
    def long_wait():
        time.sleep(20)
    request.addfinalizer(long_wait)

def test_a(timeout_fixture):
    print("hello")

def test_b():
    print("world")

def test_c():
    print("bonjour")

def test_d():
    print("monde")
```
- Run test with `--timeout-method=thread`, so pytest-timeout will kill our process and xdist will see it crashed:
```$ py.test -s -x -vv --timeout=2 --timeout-method=thread -n 1```

Result:
```
[...]
test_bug.py::test_a 
[gw0] PASSED test_bug.py::test_a 
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ Timeout ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
[...]
[gw0] node down: Not properly terminated
[gw0] FAILED test_bug.py::test_b 
Replacing crashed slave gw0
[gw1] darwin Python 2.7.10 cwd: /private/tmp/test
[gw1] Python 2.7.10 (default, Jul 30 2016, 19:40:32)  -- [GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.34)]
gw1 [4]
test_bug.py::test_d 
[gw1] PASSED test_bug.py::test_d 
test_bug.py::test_c 
[gw1] PASSED test_bug.py::test_c 
========================================================================================================================================================== FAILURES ==========================================================================================================================================================
________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ test_bug.py _________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[gw0] darwin -- Python 2.7.10 /usr/bin/python
Slave 'gw0' crashed while running 'test_bug.py::test_b'
============================================================================================================================================= 1 failed, 3 passed in 4.01 seconds =============================================================================================================================================```

`test_b` is seen as failing but hasn't been executed at all.

I believe this is due to the way xdist handles crashed items. When a node crashes, it pops the running item and reschedule it on a new node. However, when the node crashes at teardown, the running item has already been dequeued.

issue_title: Allow site override in /apps/order/utils.py:OrderCreator.create_order_model
issue_body:
Hi there!

I'm running into a site-related issue with the OrderCreator.  We are trying to pass in a site entity as an override, but the current implementation appears to ignore any provided value in favor of the Site's get_current() workflow.  Typically this wouldn't be an issue, however this is a multi-host scenario and we'd like to be able to indicate the site as part of the request to this class.  See here:

https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/apps/order/utils.py#L104

I'd like to propose that the setting of the "site" key be moved from its current location to after the "if extra_order_fields..." conditional on line 120.  We would perform a quick check to see if the site key had not been set via extra_order_fields -- if not we'd execute Site._default_manager.get_current() as is currently happening.

I am happy to make this change and submit a pull request to the django-oscar project if that's the best way to move forward?  If you could let me know that would be great.

Thanks!

Matt

issue_title: letsencrypt renew fails to renew authenticator=standalone lineage after installer=apache
issue_body:
Right now, if you do `service apache2 stop` in the hope of renewing `standalone` lineages, but the renewer decides to renew any `installer=apache` lineage, the renewer will call `.restart()` which will then cause `apache2` to start again! That, in turn, will cause TCP ports to be in use and `standalone` to be unable to renew its lineages.

The `apache` plugin should not actually "reload" a non-running Apache; there's arguably no need to do so because the new cert, if any, will be picked up automatically the next time that Apache is started.

issue_title: Way to specify dialog/gui interface
issue_body: With #2350, there isn't a way to run `letsencrypr renew` with dialog. To change this, we need a `--dialog` or `--gui` flag.

issue_title: renewer should use acme.client.Client.refresh
issue_body: `renewer.py` currently calls out to `le_client.obtain_certificate(sans)`, which does the entire authentication again and thus forces users to take down their webservers. We should use `acme.client.Client.refresh` which implements ACME Certificate Refresh.

issue_title: [Issues on Push Message]
issue_body:
Hello, Gene

Currently I’m facing several problems when trying to implement following push message use cases in client app:
1. FCM token refresh in the middle of user session
2. User logout in app

So as far as I understand, it is possible for FCM token to be refreshed. When this event occurs, we need to update our existing token on the server.

Yet in Tinode, FCM token (called `"deviceId"` in Tinode) could only be updated in `{hi}`. So in case of token refresh occurs in the middle of user session, we need to break the session first to register new updated token. 

So maybe there is a better way to do this which I'm missing?

The next issue occurs when single device is being used by multiple user. So for example User A & User B use single device together.

In Tinode, `deviceId` is associated with `uid` [(link)](https://github.com/tinode/chat/blob/devel/server/store/store.go#L430-L432), yet `store.Devices.Delete()` is only called when `deviceId` is invalid [(link)](https://github.com/tinode/chat/blob/devel/push_fcm/push_fcm.go#L150).

Since User A & User B use the device together, then the `deviceId` would be same for them. So in case User A has been logout & User B logged in, then message for User A would be still delivered to User B. 

So I think it's better if we also offers delete device functionality for user so the client could notify server which `deviceId` still used for certain user & which is not.

What do you think?

---

Also if I may suggest I think it's better if we differentiate between `deviceId` & fcm token though. The reason is because essentialy a device may have multiple tokens due to rotation / refresh. 

Since basically `store.Devices.Update()` is defined as "upsert", then every unused fcm token would be just pile up on database. But if we differentiate between `deviceId` & fcm token we could just update the associated token for the `deviceId` leaving more compact space on the database.

What do you think?

Thanks

issue_title: Contract db
issue_body:
There are two types of contracts in blockchain.
1. Contracts created by normal addresses
2. Contracts created by other contracts

The first are easy to get from node. Just get every transaction in every block and check if it creates a contract. This takes aproximatellly a day and a half

The second is complicated
You can take every transaction in every block. Check its destination address and check it code. If code is not empty, then this is contract. So you will get all contracts, created by other contracts.
But you will not get any contract, that is interacted with by other contracts only.

The complete solution is to trace every transaction in blockchain and see if it results in contract creation.

Another solution is to directly read LevelDb. Don't know if it is possible.

What way does Mythril use to build contract db?

issue_title: Capturing of ENVIRONMENT variables
issue_body:
I would like to propose a new feature to be able to link database entries and the corresponding output files on the cluster more easily. As the scheduler stores things like job_id, array_id and alike, I think it would be helpful if a user-provided list of ENV variables could be added to the database entry. Something along the lines of:

```
from sacred import Experiment
ex = Experiment("nobel prize 2020")
ex.store_env_variables(["MOAB_JOBID", "MOAB_JOBARRAYINDEX" ])
```
which would then add a key to the stored  dict like
'captured ENVIRONMENT': {"MOAB_JOBID": 123456, "MOAB_JOBARRAYINDEX": 0}

This would be useful, at least to me, to find the corresponding db entry of a logfile that indicates a problem. I know that this could be achieved by storing everything in the config manually, but I thought this might be useful in general.

Where would be a good place to start, if I were to implement that?

issue_title: Cannot Deploy to Domain Root, No Hosted Zones Found
issue_body:
We cannot to deploy to our production domain `solutions.zalando.com` due to the following error:

**ERROR: ExternalAppLoadBalancerMainDomain CREATE_FAILED: No hosted zones named zalando.com. found**

It seems senza assumes the domain follows the structure `subdomain`.`hosted_zone` and attempts to create route53 records in `hosted_zone`. This works fine in most cases, but when attempting to deploy to a domain that matches our hosted zone exactly (i.e. the root), it fails because `hosted_zone` is incorrectly set to `zalando.com`. This is consistent with @harti2006's comment on #314.

To confirm this is not just a problem with `solutions.zalando.com`, I tried deploying to the root of our other hosted zone `norris.zalan.do` with the same result.

Is there a way to deploy a stack at our domain root? The announcement of senza 2.0 listed this exact scenario as one of the reasons to move to the new A record setup, so I expect it should be possible.

More details:

- senza version: 2.0.149 
- [senza yaml used](https://github.bus.zalan.do/norris/norris-devops/blob/bugfix/28-change-solution-center-live-url/solution-center/solution-center-prod.yaml).

issue_title: Inconsistent results with QF_FP between incremental and non-incremental solver
issue_body:
I ran into some problems with floating point and NaNs. Initially I though I misunderstood how NaNs are handled in the theory of floats but it's probably a bug because the result switches between sat and unsat depending on whether I use the incremental solver or not. It looks as if the incremental solver accidentally uses floating point equality (where `!(NaN == NaN)`).

Input:
```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FP)

(declare-const x (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))

(push)
(assert (and (fp.isNaN x) (not (= x (_ NaN 11 53)))))

(check-sat) ; sat (!)
(get-model) ; x is (_ NaN 11 53)
```

Without `(push)`, the result is unsat. I've tested on recent Z3 built from a7a0deed3f.

Running Z3 on the following sequence of propositions

issue_title: possible bug in z3 ddnf implementation
issue_body:
There is some difference between the ddnf algorithm described in [ddnf's paper](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/wp-content/uploads/2015/11/An-Efficient-Data-Structure-for-Header-Spaces.pdf) and the z3 implementation of it.
Due to [this line](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/cd24535e51263d8447efea9b917977863b97d8e5/src/muz/ddnf/ddnf.cpp#L195) in the the implementation, the data structure that is created after inserting a list of ternary bit vectors (tbvs) might be different from what is described in the paper. 
For example, after inserting `11`, `1*`, `*1`, the expected data structure according to the paper (or at least my understanding of it) should look like this:
```
      xx
    /    \
   x1    1x
    \    /
      11
```

However, here is the result of calling `display` function on `ddnf_core` after inserting the above tbvs (in the given order): 
```
node[0: xx 2 3]
node[1: 11]
node[2: x1 1]
node[3: 1x]
```
As you can see the implementation does not add node 1 as a child on node 3 even though its tbv is a a subset of the tbv of that node.  

If I insert the tbvs in the reverse order, (`*1`, `1*`, `11`), the result is as expected:
```
node[0: xx 1 2]
node[1: x1 3]
node[2: 1x 3]
node[3: 11]
```

I looks like a bug in the implementation to me. I was wondering whether that is indeed the case or there something that I am missing?

As part of my work in PR #1289 I'm trying to run Z3's unit tests under ASan.

After fixing some trivial leaks (PR #1301) I hit this leak

```

    #1 0xbe2af0 in memory::allocate(unsigned long) /home/user/z3_src/src/util/memory_manager.cpp:276:16

I took a look (in [`tst_nlsat()`](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/master/src/test/nlsat.cpp#L701)) and saw that there were calls to `exit()` that I'm pretty sure shouldn't be there. These prevent running further tests and might also explain the reported leak. If we exit there the destructor for `timeit` is not going to get called and so memory allocated in `timeit` will not get freed.

The commit that added these calls to `exit()` is 20bbdfe31a7e8ceb72c49ee5f2dbff112825801f . @NikolajBjorner is there a reason for these calls to `exit()`?

-    exit(0);

issue_title: Smearing issue with TransformerLayer
issue_body:
It seems to me as if the pixels that lie on the boundary of the input to the TransformerLayer are smeared in the output.  I noticed this when I was playing around with spatial transformers by transforming MNIST digits and trying to let the transformer module learn to map the digit back to its original pose.

In the image below, the first row shows the input, the second row shows the output of the spatial transformer, and the third row shows the output of the spatial transformer after the boundary of the input has been set to zero.  Notice that in the second row smearing occurs wherever the input digit was touching the boundary, whereas the third row does not exhibit this effect.
![smearing](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6610704/15592112/145fe6c6-236f-11e6-9168-f484a225141d.png)

I suspect that the issue arises when the coordinates are [clipped](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/8fe645d28b66f991d547e9b6a314251b8e84446a/lasagne/layers/special.py#L470), because this leads to several locations in the output grid all mapping back to the same boundary location in the  input.

Here is some code to reproduce the issue.  You can get a sample of transformed MNIST digits [here](https://www.dropbox.com/s/ayxt5jkxtvekri3/transformed_mnist.pickle?dl=0).

```
import cv2
import cPickle as pickle
import numpy as np
import theano
import theano.tensor as T
from lasagne.layers import get_output
from lasagne.layers import DenseLayer
from lasagne.layers import InputLayer
from lasagne.layers import TransformerLayer
from lasagne.init import Constant
from lasagne.nonlinearities import linear

with open('transformed_mnist.pickle', 'rb') as f:
    X = pickle.load(f)
Y = np.copy(X)
Y[:, :, 0, :] = 0
Y[:, :, :, 0] = 0
Y[:, :, -1, :] = 0
Y[:, :, :, -1] = 0

l_in = InputLayer((None, 1, 28, 28))

M = np.array([[1.5, 0, 0.0], [0, 1.5, 0.0]])
l_loc = DenseLayer(
    l_in, num_units=6, W=Constant(0.0), b=M.flatten(), nonlinearity=linear
)
l_trans = TransformerLayer(l_in, l_loc)

X_tensor = T.tensor4()
X_hat = get_output(l_trans, X_tensor)
infer = theano.function([X_tensor], X_hat)

output1 = infer(X)
output2 = infer(Y)
row1 = (255. * np.hstack(X.transpose(0, 2, 3, 1)))
row2 = (255. * np.hstack(output1.transpose(0, 2, 3, 1)))
row3 = (255. * np.hstack(output2.transpose(0, 2, 3, 1)))
cv2.imwrite('smearing.png', np.vstack((row1, row2, row3)))
```

issue_title: DilatedConv2DLayer: dilation or dilation-1
issue_body:
In the DilatedConv2DLayer docstring, for the `dilation` parameter [it says](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/layers/conv.py#L812-L814): 
```
An integer or a 2-element tuple specifying the dilation factor of the filters, 
i.e., the number of zeros inserted between filter elements
```
Which implies that a dilation factor of e.g. 1 would insert 1 zero between every pair of filter elements. However, the note at the bottom [says](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/layers/conv.py#L868-L874):
```
The dilated convolution is implemented as the backward pass of a
convolution wrt. weights, passing the filters as the output gradient.
It can be thought of as dilating the filters (by adding ``dilation - 1``
zeros between adjacent filter elements) and cross-correlating them with the
input. See [1]_ for more background.
```
Which implies that a dilation factor of 1 would result in no zeros being added between filter elements.

Looking at the code I think the latter is what actually happens (?), but it would be helpful if this were clarified in the docstring for the dilation parameter.

issue_title: The output size of TransposedConv2DLayer(Deconv2DLayer)
issue_body:
My input size is (14,14) and I want the output size to be (28,28) after implementating TransposedConv2DLayer. Here is an example of code:

network = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(shape=(None, 1, 14, 14), input_var=input_var)
network = lasagne.layers.Deconv2DLayer(network, num_filters=1, filter_size=(3, 3), crop='same',
            stride=2,  nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.rectify)

But the actual output size is (27,27).
Then I read the source code which defines the output size of TransposedConv2DLayer:
(output_length - 1) * stride - 2 * pad + filter_size         (1)

In this example  “output_length” is 14. To get my expected output size, for simplicity, I just change (1) into:
(output_length - 1) * stride - 2 * pad + filter_size + 1   (2)

And it works. But I don't know whether this change is reasonable( I guess the back propagation can't pass through the added dimension). Is there better way to solve this kind of problem?

issue_title: Segmentation fault (core dumped) after Conv2D-Layer
issue_body:
Hey!

Recently, I encountered a weird problem when using `lasagne` and `theano`. The following minimal example reproduces the error for me:
```python
import numpy as np
import theano
import theano.tensor as T
import lasagne

batch_size = 1

data = np.random.rand(10, 3, 425, 620).astype(np.float32)
W = np.random.rand(3, 3, 65, 65).astype(np.float32)

input_var = T.tensor4(name='input', dtype='float32')

net = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(input_var=input_var, shape=(batch_size, ) + data.shape[1:])
net = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(net, num_filters=W.shape[0], filter_size=W.shape[-2:], 
                                                    pad='valid', W=W, b=None,
                                                    nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.linear)

filtered_array = np.zeros(net.output_shape)
out_var = lasagne.layers.get_output(net)

conv_fun = theano.function([input_var], outputs=out_var)

for id_batch in xrange(0, data.shape[0], batch_size):
    print '{}'.format(id_batch)
    filtered_array[id_batch:id_batch + batch_size] = conv_fun(data[id_batch:id_batch + batch_size])
```
(The specific parameters are chosen in a way to mimic my actual problem)
Running this script results in a `Segmentation fault (core dumped)` for the first batch already.

What seems strange is that the whole routine was working before without any problems. 
I reproduced the error on my laptop with 16GB RAM, Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3230M CPU @ 2.60GHz on Ubuntu 14.04.3 LTS (64bit), on a PC with 16GB RAM, Intel(R) Core(TM) i3 CPU 530 @ 2.93GHz on Ubuntu 14.04.3 LTS (64bit) (running `theano` on CPU) and an Amazon EC2 GPU unit (using the GPU).

I'm using the newest `theano` version ('0.8.0.dev0.dev-RELEASE') and newest `lasagne` version ('0.2.dev1'). 

The error seems to me like the system runs out of memory but the whole thing **was** working for me without any problems with older versions of `theano` and `lasagne`. 

Do you have any suggestions how I can make it workable again?
